source: Claude gap analysis 2026-06-11 (PROJECT_MAP.md addendum, Track B)
title: Streaming responses: tokens print as they generate (root + normie)
current_state: router.chat() blocks until the full response exists, then run() prints it in one shot. On long answers Ash stares at a silent prompt for 10-30s. Every modern agent CLI streams.
target_state: router.chat(stream=True) yields text deltas for providers that support it (Anthropic SSE, Groq/Cerebras OpenAI-style stream); the REPL prints deltas as they arrive. Non-streaming providers and the tool-use loop fall back to blocking (tool_use blocks need the complete message anyway). Final LLMResponse object is identical to today so logging/cost paths are untouched.
migration_plan: 1. Add stream support to the Anthropic provider first (root mode, biggest latency win): client.messages.stream, accumulate into the same LLMResponse shape.
2. Thread an optional on_delta callback through router.chat; pi_agent.run() passes print-flush; all other callers omit it (zero behavior change).
4. Streaming only for the FINAL text segment: if stop_reason=tool_use, suppress delta printing for that round (tool loop unchanged).
5. Tests: fake provider emitting deltas — assert accumulated text == blocking text, callback receives all deltas, tool_use rounds never stream.
risk_notes: Touches pi_agent.py + llm_router (RISK_FLAGGED → interactive session, not sprint.py). Keep the blocking path as default; streaming is opt-in via callback so every existing test passes unchanged.
